name: kimiflow
description: Context-aware Kimiflow launcher, active-session feature/fix loop for Codex with mechanical plan-blocker gates, code-review ensembles, existing-feature checks, project intelligence maps, token-cheap memory/learning recall, Obsidian Terminal MCP setup, health/auth status, and Vault sync handoffs.

interface.shortDescription: Understand a codebase, plan changes, check features, and run feature/fix work through gates
interface.longDescription: Kimiflow opens with a context-aware launcher when invoked without a concrete task, then routes the user into project mapping, memory/recall curation, Obsidian auto-detect/connect, Terminal-based Obsidian MCP setup, Vault health/auth checks, Vault prefetch/sync handoffs, findings, backlog resume, existing-feature checks, feature work, bug fixes, improvement analysis, or docs. A project-local active-session contract keeps follow-up requests inside the current Kimiflow run until it is finished, parked, failed, or aborted, with run-local item tracking and stale-state revalidation before finish. The Terminal wizard lets the user enter the Obsidian API key outside chat, writes host config, and keeps secrets out of .kimiflow/. It also runs a disciplined feature and bug-fix loop in Codex, and can build a local project intelligence map before coding. It maps the codebase, architecture, conventions, tests, flows, stale sections, docs, opt-in improvement/refactoring ideas, and bounded project memory under .kimiflow/project/. Normal coding runs use that map and memory recall to understand what already exists before planning, then continue through a mechanical plan-blocker gate, evidence-based verification, a focused code-review ensemble with orchestrator-verified findings, and the commit gate. Existing-feature checks review behavior, frontend/backend or command/API wiring, contracts, state/data, tests, and docs/security without editing code.
defaultPrompt: Open kimiflow and show project status
Review this change with the kimiflow ensemble
Check an already-built feature with kimiflow
Map and understand this codebase with kimiflow
Refresh kimiflow project memory and recall
Open Obsidian MCP Terminal setup with kimiflow
Connect and check Obsidian memory health with kimiflow
Find architecture and refactoring opportunities with kimiflow
